commit.message:
Getting started (#33)

* Add GettingStarted v1.0 and v1.1 components

Introduce two onboarding feature variants (GettingStarted_1_0 and GettingStarted_1_1). Each variant includes a React TSX component, corresponding CSS, and an index export. The components provide scaffolded TypeScript props and basic onboarding forms/step UI; v1.1 adds textarea-based inputs and step-complete styling. Update main.tsx to import the new components and render GettingStarted_1_1 in place of App for immediate preview.

* Add GettingStarted onboarding components

Introduce GettingStarted feature screens for versions 1.2, 2.0, 3.0, 3.1, 3.2 and 3.3. Each version adds a TSX component, scoped CSS, and an index export; components include scaffolded TypeScript props and accessible markup for onboarding flows (company info, products upload, persona selection/sample post, results). Minor update to src/main.tsx to integrate the new screens.

* Revamp GettingStarted UI styles and mount component

Overhauled styles for GettingStarted_1_0: updated theme variables, refreshed header gradient, refined spacing/typography, improved form grid and input focus states, added responsive breakpoints, introduced .gs-step-dots and .gs-submit-row, and restyled the primary continue button. Updated GettingStarted_1_0.tsx: removed scaffold comments, added step-dots markup, adjusted email label and wrapped the Continue button in a submit row. Adjusted global layout in index.css to remove fixed root width/centering and border. Updated main.tsx to render GettingStarted_1_0 (other feature mounts commented out).

* Refactor GettingStarted layouts and styles

Unified and modernized the GettingStarted feature across versions (1_0–3_3): replaced legacy class structure with a consistent container/header/main/sidebar/form pattern, updated gradients, spacing, typography, form controls and responsive rules, and standardized button styles. Updated TSX markup for improved semantics (label htmlFor/ids), restructured sidebar steps, and standardized class names. Added basic interactivity in GettingStarted_1_1 (useState for About Us and Target Audience, focus handlers to clear default text). Also added a .hintrc to relax a few accessibility hints during development.

* fix: import ChangeEvent from react instead of using React.ChangeEvent namespace

fix: use consistent per-product VWAP benchmark across all metrics (#16)

profit_factor, daily_pnl(), best/worst trade selection, and the HTML
report trade table were all benchmarking fills against the portfolio-level
VWAP whilst total_alpha_eur used per-product VWAPs. This caused logically
contradictory output (e.g. +784 EUR Total PnL alongside Profit Factor 0.00).

Changes:
- compute_fill_pnl(): add optional product_vwaps dict; uses per-product
  benchmark when provided, falls back to portfolio VWAP
- compute_profit_factor(): thread product_vwaps through to compute_fill_pnl
- BacktestResult: add product_vwaps field (populated for IDC runs)
- BacktestResult.daily_pnl(): use product_vwaps via compute_fill_pnl
- BacktestResult.summary(): best/worst trade display uses product_vwaps
- BacktestEngine: pass per_product_vwap to compute_profit_factor and
  best/worst fill selection; store on BacktestResult
- report._top_trades_rows(): use result.product_vwaps

Tests:
- TestComputeFillPnl: per-product lookup, fallback to portfolio VWAP
- TestComputeProfitFactor: consistent benchmark, no-losses case
- test_profit_factor_consistent_with_total_pnl_sign: end-to-end IDC
  assertion that profit_factor and total_alpha_eur cannot contradict

commit.message:
fix: apply IGNORECASE to single-char FS in split and field splitting

The single-character FS fast paths in split_string_by_field_separator
and split_fields_into bypassed regex matching entirely, ignoring the
IGNORECASE flag. When IGNORECASE=1, fall through to the regex path so
case-insensitive matching is applied.

Fixes parity failure: 028_ignorecase.awk

sync: release v1.7.8 orchestration stabilization (#26)

* fix: use NPM OIDC trusted publisher instead of token

The publish workflow was failing with NODE_AUTH_TOKEN empty because
the token-based publish is deprecated in favor of NPM OIDC trusted
publishers. Fix:

- Remove NODE_AUTH_TOKEN env var (conflicts with OIDC)
- Upgrade npm to latest (>=11.5.1) for OIDC publishing support
- Keep id-token: write permission and --provenance flag

NPM trusted publisher is already configured on npmjs.com for this
repo. OIDC federates identity from GitHub Actions → NPM, eliminating
the need for long-lived tokens.

* fix: payload passthrough for wing/room/original_text/valid_until in retrieval pipeline

Root cause: hybrid_search._vector_search() and memory_retrieval.retrieve_context()
were both hardcoded to cherry-pick only content/type/project/tags/timestamp from
Qdrant payload results. All spatial, temporal, and zero-loss fields were silently
dropped. Also fixed retrieve command threshold default (0.7 -> 0.45) and removed
stale imports causing UnboundLocalError in contradiction resolver.

* fix: complete contradiction resolver pipeline

Two root causes prevented contradiction resolution from working:
1. Empty query string in retrieve_context() produced zero-score vectors
   that fell below threshold, so no existing facts were ever found.
   Fix: use the new content as the search query.
2. Deprecated facts (valid_until set) were never filtered during search.
   Fix: add must_not range filter in hybrid_search._vector_search() to
   auto-exclude points where valid_until <= current time.

* fix: BM25 results now respect Qdrant payload filters (project/wing/room)

BM25 (SQLite FTS5) has no concept of Qdrant payload filters, so unscoped
results leaked into hybrid search when: 1) BM25-only results merged into
vector results without validation, and 2) the BM25-only fallback path
(when vector returns 0) returned results without any filter check.

Fix: Both paths now validate each BM25-only result against Qdrant via
a point lookup before including it. Also fixed the merge path to pass
wing/room/original_text/valid_until through the merged result dict.

commit.message:
fix(analysis): show inlay hints for inferred closure param and return types

Add `has_type_annotation` to FunctionParameter and `has_return_type` to
FunctionDeclaration so inlay hints can distinguish user-written annotations
from type-checker-inferred ones. Previously, bidirectional closure inference
filled in type_annotation/return_type, causing the hint collector to skip
them (it assumed they were user-written).

The hint collector now:
- Filters out unresolved `Var` types (e.g. `?10002`) from hints
- Checks decl.return_type first (written back by type checker) before
  falling back to type table or body expression type

The type checker also writes back the resolved return type to
`decl.return_type` for closures and top-level functions when the user
didn't annotate a return type.

Fix settings lost on machine restart and update disable not persisting

Root cause: App and MainViewModel each created their own SettingsService
instance, so the VM's saves used a stale default AppSettings object that
overwrote fields not covered by SyncViewModelToSettings (like
IsUpdateCheckEnabled, UpdateSnoozedUntil, LastUpdateCheck).

Fixes:
- Share single SettingsService between App and MainViewModel
- Save window position immediately on drag end (not just OnExit)
- Debounced save on MainWindow resize
- Use IsFirstRun flag instead of fragile != 0 check for position restore
- Remove debug MessageBox left in first-run path
- Replace runtime-dependent SystemParameters default for WindowTop with NaN
- Use atomic temp-file write in SaveSettings to prevent corruption

- parse_liq column naming: cosmetic, generic names are fine

feat: queue user input while LLM is generating, merge and submit on TurnComplete

When status.thinking == true and user presses Enter with plain text:
- Input goes into App::queued_inputs Vec instead of submitting immediately
- Status bar shows '📥 N queued' next to the thinking spinner
- A system message '📥 Queued (N pending — will send when LLM finishes)' is shown

On AgentNotification::TurnComplete:
- All queued inputs are joined with double newlines into one merged message
- The merged message is submitted as a single client.submit() call
- queue is cleared; Ctrl+C/Esc abort also clears the queue

Slash commands (starting with /) bypass the queue and are always handled
immediately (including /abort which clears the queue).

commit.message:
fix: security audit — race condition, PID overflow, UTF-8 panic, input validation (#5)

CRITICAL: locks::acquire() now uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE to prevent TOCTOU race
between concurrent file lock acquisitions.

MEDIUM: PID cast overflow in agents.rs and locks.rs — replaced unsafe
`pid as i32` with `i32::try_from(pid)` + bounds check.

MEDIUM: process_scanner::truncate() now uses char_indices() to avoid
panicking on multi-byte UTF-8 boundaries.

MEDIUM: Added input length validation on send, register-agent, and
context-set endpoints (agent name ≤128, content ≤64K, key ≤256).

ADR-002 documents all findings. ADR-008 copied from monorepo.
